Hey everyone, my name is Russell Brunson. I'm here with Robbie Summers and We're excited to bring you guys some really cool stuff. This is obviously as you know the millionaire incubator training course And what we're gonna be talking about In this DVD and throughout the whole course is basically kind of how to set up your own little mini call center and some of the background We set up our first call center. How many years ago was that now? Gosh, it's been about five six years ago I was six years ago. Yeah. Yeah, we decided to launch a call center We started it started building it and met Robbie had a ton of experienced students So we hired and brought him up and within a year and a half or so we built a huge sales floor with over 60 people doing well over a hundred thousand dollars a week and had Just made a ton of money together and then economy shifted We decided that we didn't want to call center and the whole thing kind of went away and for for a couple years we didn't do any kind of back-end phone sales at all and and After a couple years, I really missed that part of our business miss the the hundreds of thousands of dollars a week We're missing out on and thought how can we do this again? but without having the the 60 sales people and and we spent a lot of time and and On my side I spent a lot of time working on the front end like how do we create and structure? A funnel to really weed out all the people so we can do similar numbers with a lot less people And we had something built that I thought was pretty good And we set it up and we ran it for about a month. We generated about a hundred leads or so We had two sales people trying this thing out. I took out our old scripts We had we dustin them off and they went to work and in an entire month They closed one deal and I was like man This is harder than I remember being and so I called Robbie up one day and I was like hey man Just was curious if you'd be willing to come in and spend a couple hours training the two little sales people we have on the old scripts that we were using and how it you know and see what could happen and Luckily Robbie came in Spend about four hours with our with our little mini sales team and the next day They did thirty six thousand dollars in sales next week did sixty seven thousand dollars in sales and we haven't looked back since and so And again, we're doing those kind of numbers with a very small team And that's kind of what this whole product came about everyone started asking us like like what are you doing? How are you doing the similar numbers that you were doing 60 people with with with two or three people? And that's why we decided to put this course together So we're grateful I'm grateful have Robbie here and we're gonna be going through this this DVD this part of it We're gonna be talking about All the call center setup like and so this TV is really for you the entrepreneur The other DVDs are gonna be a lot more specific to The sales people and we'll talk about that as we go through but Robbie Really a great way to help you guys build and grow your business and to make additional money and as Russell was talking about one of the things that we did initially is we had a huge call center and egos and they all think they need to be doing things their own way and And it's it can be an incredible headache For it was for Russell for me for our entire team To be able to build that and grow that and so here you know We've got a picture of a huge call center and this is exactly what we don't want to have Is is a bunch of people trying to try to do things their own way a bunch of egos going their own way Just a very small Directed group of people that can produce big numbers and the great thing about that is that for you as the entrepreneur It's gonna make you a lot of money and it's also gonna give that opportunity for just those two people To make a lot of money as well And so it really creates a great win-win situation for everybody that's involved with this mini call center Now what one of the things that we're gonna talk about today and I'm gonna talk about Vince Lombardi He's one of my one of my all-time heroes I loved playing football did a lot of different things with that But Vince Lombardi for anybody who doesn't know was probably the greatest NFL football coach of all time and When he was Coach in the Green Bay Packers. He was a head coach of the Green Bay Packers We've got professional football players the very best football players in the world at that time at the beginning of Training camp he would walk in To the room With these professional players, you know, they played pop-warner football They played high school and junior high football and college football been playing football in the NFL for a number of years And he'd walk in and he'd say now notice. I've got my Boise State football Russell and I were I was gonna bring a full-size football, but I didn't have one those Boise State So so Russell and I were big Boise State fans But Vince Lombardi would walk in to the room with the football players and he'd say hey guys This is a football and he would go and he'd walk out and he'd say this is the football field These are the goalposts This is the goal line and he would start with the very very basics of teaching people how to play football and Which seems really odd because some of you guys might have a lot of experience already doing sales and Even with that said maybe even you guys have some sales guys That's good. That's wonderful We're gonna start with some very basic things We're gonna talk about things that are gonna help you to build and grow your business And maybe even some things that if you've already done a little bit of a sales Call center or you've already got some sales guys Maybe some things to to go back and to to take a look at or some things to add into your current process And if you don't have it if if you don't have a clue and and I'm sitting here and saying hey This is a football or hey, this is how you set up a call center We're right in the right place right there So the very first thing that that we're gonna talk about that setting up a call center It's not a difficult thing to do if you have the right things put in place that there's some bare essentials some Necessities some things that you've got to have the very first thing is you've got to have leads and Russell is gonna talk about this. You know in in he talks about it in the other DVDs But but the way that Russell is driving in leads and and he's taught you guys how to drive in leads is Incredible because what it's doing is it's weeding out what what took us, you know 60 guys and 3000 leads Now it's hey We're getting 30 40 leads a week to three guys handle that and and are able to produce the same amount of money because They're weeding all the people that aren't good out in the on the front end And so it's incredible what Russell is doing with these leads and and how they're how they're driving them in now This is gonna sound silly Next thing you need you need phones guys I was calling leads once for somebody and Went went and talked to him and said hey, I need phone numbers on these leads You need leads with phone numbers. You need you need phones We we are calling out to people again. We're gonna be talking about some things that they're gonna be pretty basic The next thing that you've got to have and and I think this is critical. There's a lot of different headsets that you can get You know that this is a headset that I picked up At staples for for 20 or 30 bucks. You can get more advanced ones get Wireless headsets that that's actually what I use most of the time as a wireless headset Something that that keeps me in the zone of who I'm talking to and that they can hear me and communicate and then I can move around I've called on the phone before where I've got to hold that up the the receiver to my ear and that's a difficult thing to do because I want I'm very animated and Even though they can't see me on the other end of the phone They can feel me and and they can understand that I'm passionate and I'm emotional about the things that I'm doing a Headset allows me to be able to do that The the last thing that you've got to have and and this is something that is really really critical Is you've got to have superstar salespeople? You know and there's some some very particular ways that we can talk about how to drive in and find those superstar sales people That that's really what we're gonna talk about But but in order to have those salespeople you've got to have some things that are set up and in place and I call these Specific measurable results Steven our cubby kind of talks about always having results being results driven and the things that you're looking for As as you're building and growing this that your call center you want to create a compelling scorecard People and especially salespeople they play differently When they're keeping score, I don't know if you guys have ever been you know out playing with your kids and You know, maybe you go bowling and you say gosh, we're not gonna keep score today It doesn't matter but but if I go Russell when we go bowling, okay, we're keeping score. Aren't we? Russell if you and I if we we've gone down to the mat and maybe wasn't the map we've wrestled a couple of times and we We I know how many times you've thrown me around and you know how many times that we Yeah, I Saw people like I've wrestled like years and years ago and in my head. I still know like if I'm up or down I'm they have a big score. It's so critical Because we're gonna talk about this the types of people that you want to salespeople They they're going to have that mentality that attitude and people react differently When when you're keeping score so your scoreboard And I'm gonna show you a picture of what your scoreboard should look like that There's a few things that it should do on a weekly basis a monthly basis and an annual basis, okay? You need to know where we're at right now today What where am I at on Monday Tuesday Wednesday? Where am I at monthly? Where am I at annually? I want to know where I'm at what have accomplished what I've achieved and how far ahead or how far behind I am the next guy In front of me or behind me. It's important for them to see that and to have it in a place That's visual that they can know and understand what that looks like The second thing is where we want to be You know we can say only to talk about goals and we can do a whole training and spend days talking about goals And how to set goals the fact the matter is is that that they have to know and understand what it is That you're looking for where they want to be and what they want to accomplish and When we need to be there. It's got to be specific. We've got to have those measurable results So this scorecard the scoreboard and this something you get set up and you get it in place before you even hire somebody Is it should be someplace? It's visible Identify if things go off track and how to get them back on track if corrective action need to be taken now Russell You remember that you know, you remember our boards and what they look like I do remember that our call center if you look on the left-hand side We've got you know everybody and and we had we had a number of these boards that were hanging up around Around around our office with the 60 guys and each and every day we would come in and this is what I produced on Monday This is what I produced on Tuesday throughout the entire week. This is where the company is at This is what my goal is that would be on the right side for that week And we had a whole nother separate scoreboard that that we would use as far as where people were at Annually and monthly and would break it down so that they knew how and why they were producing and Russell you remember when when we had that that sales floor when you would walk down on the sales floor What would everybody want to show you and their numbers? They're there. Let me show you chase that look at the scorecard Russell look at the scorecard They want to see it They they want other people to see it and and they want to brag about it and and having the scorecard Right up there in front of everybody is a great thing now on the flip side to somebody whose numbers aren't great and Say it's a halfway through the week gosh all of a sudden they see everybody else on the floor is doing great They're embarrassed and whatever it takes to get those numbers up They're willing to do it and so having a scorecard and and this is this is very simple very easy to look at You know we we've got our our blue and orange tape is what we use Just on a white board that we got it. I think we got a little deeper. Yeah, they were cheap yeah And and and every week we would erase it and then we'd keep an Excel spreadsheet and we'd post that up as far as where people were On an annual basis and monthly and things of that nature We'd call it a six week rolling average where they're at and those are the things that we would look at because Either either I know I need to do better because I see everybody else in the company is doing great Or or I'm doing great and I'm top and I can you know top guy and I can walk around With my chest puffed out and and when the owner comes down. I'm like hey guy look at this. I'm number one You know that right? That it's so critical to have that scorecard and have that up in front of everybody for them to see and understand Where we're going the other great thing too is is especially after you've hired a couple of people there's nothing better than looking at hiring that third person and Walking in and saying hey look, you know Robbie this week he's made $2,000 because this is our commission scale and Kimberly she's made 1500 bucks and Troy's made 3000 bucks this week all of a sudden what it does is as new people come in It's not just a so you're telling you that you make a lot of money No, I'm showing you that people here make a lot of money and it reinforces that In their mind when you have a new guy come in into the office That's one of the big keys. I think of finding superstars is a superstar doesn't really want to normally come in like build a new thing They want to come in to be like wow this is I can tap into this and jump right in and start making money today And is there anything better than immediate validation? Yeah, and and to sit down and say so who's this who's this Robbie guy? Well, he he's that guy over there and he he barely graduated from high school in Emmett, Idaho Okay, and and he he's making 3000 bucks this week Okay, or whatever it is that that people see that and they look at it and say well if that guy can do it I can do it that that immediate validation it comes in and by having that scorecard that scoreboard put up somewhere Whether you've got one sales guy you look at where they're at where they want to be and what we need to do to get on course Or or whether you've got 60 sales guys That that if they look at that and they see that and they know where the scorecard is at it makes a world of difference Because before we go on I want to talk really quick for a second about One of the core things I think that drives motivation and gets people throughout the week moving moving forward and it's kind of The way we used to do our sliding sales Structure and we still do it today And I think it's one of the most powerful ways to really motivate people and to get them To continue to produce week-in week out and hit the goals and numbers that all of us want I'd love for you to kind of talk about that concept. Well the best way to do that Russell. It's with the Commission scale that you have with with your your salespeople and every time you do a Commission scale that that what happens is it's a sliding Commission scale and every single week your Commission scale it starts over What one of the best things about about the industry and one of the worst things too because you have a great week And and all of a sudden that that's all erased and you're starting back But but gosh, you know even even top performers occasionally have a bad week and and you know It's sure nice to be able to erase that as well The the bad week but the thing that drives them is that things are paid On a sliding scale and and so what would happen is I would start out You know each and every week at zero and at a 10% Commission and as I make sales my my Commission would increase And it would be retroactive So let's say that first of all I'm gonna get $5,000 in sales and from zero to $5,000 in sales I get paid 10% or I make 500 bucks okay From from 5,000 to 6,000 and it'd be different for everybody, but but it goes to 12% So now all the sudden that entire $6,000 I get paid 12% and and it would cap out at 20% Now the thing about it is is that all of a sudden what what motivates a top performer is I know that when I'm at a 20% Commission each and every dollar that I make from now on Russell's paying me more money right On on the and I know that on Monday I come in and I'm starting back at 10% again Okay, I need things to be able to motivate me. So all of a sudden now guess what happens. I'm Let's say I've maxed out my commission on a Thursday. Well, I'm making 20% You know, I put up $20,000 in sales. I've made $4,000 that week, okay But but I know that one more sale, okay a $5,000 sale pays me a thousand bucks now Whereas on Monday that same that same $5,000 sale. Guess what? It only pays me 500 bucks So now all of a sudden my Friday my Thursday my Friday and Saturday I come in and work twice as hard because I want to make sure that I capitalize on that commission That's there that's available And so it's one of the best ways to be able to really motivate your sales guys is make sure that you've got things on a sliding scale Make sure that every single week You start that over if you don't it builds up and all of a sudden You're paying everybody 20-25% on every single sale that they make whereas You know that you're just not getting the motivation for them to come in and for them to work hard because they know that gosh next Monday I'm gonna make a thousand bucks on that sale regardless if I do it today or tomorrow and Obviously, we all know that having money today is better than having money tomorrow You know, right, right? Let me get my calculator out and make sure but but but we know that and so that that drives and that motivates your sales guys to Come in and to push harder and harder each and every week Yeah, I think it's interesting too like when we relaunched our mini colson we set up our structure and had everything going and The the very first week that that after you had trained them and they had this big huge spike in volume they were excited they didn't look close enough for the numbers and and Kimberly ended up missing her 20% commission by like 500 bucks and she's like, oh, it's not a big deal and then we stop and I know it is a big deal Let's look at the math and we showed her the math and if she would have closed one even a small like a thousand dollar deal She would have made an extra like 1400 bucks for the entire week because of the increase in commission and that's the lesson that after they learned once they never forget it And now it's like every day they're looking at that They're like man if I got one more deal my instead of me and we have made a little spreadsheet where they can type in and see What it looks like like if I make a five thousand I do what I do boom also like oh I make an extra 800 bucks like for the entire week And suddenly what happens is like I said Thursday Friday Saturday. They're going crazy and Saturday They're here dialing and they're looking at time zones and who's still awake. Oh, someone's awake in Australia I'm dying because if I get one more deal man, it's an extra thousand bucks I can I get you know, it becomes huge for them and and then they said Monday restets over and and it starts the process again and it's I think it's one of the big driving forces of how you really get the consistent volume you want. It's kind of like In college, I don't know if you ever donated plasma. I wasn't big enough But my roommate was big into plasma and the very first time you go and they give you like 15 bucks And the second time they give you like 35 bucks then the third time they give you like 10 But in the fourth time it's like 45 And so he kept coming back because the next time is the big hit the next time is like I get the big payment And then we sit back over to 10 but it kept him consistently going in every single week because he knew that no matter where he was at the next The next one was the big one and so it kept motivating keep going in and and for a year He's probably still this day as far as I know He was giving us plasma all the time because because the way that the the payment structure was was built out So I think it's a big important part for everyone to play with it. It absolutely makes a huge difference and Your D&I personalities are driven and motivated because of this type of a commission structure This is something that's helping them to tap into that that driver that dominant, you know trait that they have It helps push and it helps drive in that in that direction So that's something that that's really really important to To know and understand That as you do that it builds and drives right people in there So that's one of the first things you want to get set up along with your phones your headsets The next thing you need to be in a position that with those specific measurable results that you can look at what the statistics are and Statistics are critical to be able to track and understand what's happening on a sales floor There's a few things that we tracked that that were really really important obviously number one is overall sales volume If somebody's numbers were where they were supposed to be The rest of the things that I list out here nobody cared Okay, about what what the people you know as far as the statistics and the results now somebody's sales volume If it wasn't where it's supposed to be what what we want to do is we want to go down We want to look at what what else is happening there So so we look at how much time is spent on the phone, okay? number of dials being made each day and and also obviously the time on premise the time at work you know Where where are they spending their time and what's happening when when they're on the on the sales floor? There's a lot of different ways to track this, you know with a phone system You know Russell when we had our big sales floor. We we had a fabulous phone system, didn't we? Okay, okay a little bit of money a lot a lot of money, okay? And and the thing of it is is that with a phone line driven into your office? From Vonage you can get all these these numbers too. Okay, you can no one understand how many dials somebody's made How long they've been on the phone? You can have those numbers pulled right out immediately. You can also do it I do a lot of calling through Google voice and you know if you take a look at this this Google voice Screen that we have here, you know, you can know exactly in a week how many calls that I've made, you know Over the course of time that I've used this particular Google account. I've made almost 14,000 phone calls Out of this out of this Google voice account I know and understand how much time I've spent, you know I spent 24 minutes on the phone with this phone number and six minutes with that phone number and one minute with that phone number That I can have those results pulled up immediately and know right where my sales people are at So whatever the phone system that you set up and again It can be something as simple as having somebody with a Gmail account tied into Google voice and an accorded headset You can get those results It's not a difficult thing to have those statistics in front of you because we want to make sure that we're giving our sales people every opportunity to be successful Sales people need structure They they can't just be thrown out there and just say hey Here's a stack of leads go and run with it. Don't let them convince you otherwise They're good sales people. They'll try and convince you of it. They're sales people, okay? And and you know gosh caught the American way What whatever one they want to get away with doing what the least amount is possible, okay? They've got to have structure that they need to have that and what happens is when they have that structure They they really thrive and so make sure that you set your expectations with your sales people right from day one Set a schedule based upon the most effective hours to call now With each individual, you know business owner that we're dealing with We're gonna take a look at this and we'll give you some suggestions exactly when your sales people should be calling for us Because we weren't calling business to business the very best hours for us Were to be calling from nine o'clock in the morning till twelve o'clock in the morning It was amazing how many people we would contact in the morning that wanted to make money, okay? And then what we would do is we would take a three-hour break Remember we're in mountain time, okay? So so at three o'clock we would come back and that would be five o'clock east coast time and we would come in We would work from three o'clock to eight o'clock at night So we'd put in our last five hours at that time frame. So we would be calling from five o'clock, you know till nine o'clock east coast time and by the time we leave from seven o'clock to eight o'clock It would be seven o'clock, California time Pacific time and and we would try to catch the people at home at that point Now if you're selling business to business with with your product and again, this is something that will will work with you specifically on But but if you're selling business to business you want to be talking to people during business hours There's no reason for you to keep your guys around till eight o'clock at night because nobody's gonna be there unless you're east coast and You're trying to tap into those people on the west coast So so we'll talk about that but but take a look at your schedule and when the people that you deal with Are gonna be around they're gonna be in front of their phone that they're gonna be available and and set your schedule Along those lines hold them accountable for you for their results now just to give you a little bit of an example of what we did our expectations when when On our big sales for and these are great expectations As far as benchmarks that people needed to make needed to hit we we wanted to produce $7,000 Minimum in personal sales each and every week the reason we want them to do that is in Boise, Idaho The Commission scales Commission structure that we were on it provided them with a good lifestyle. They were happy They were excited to come into work. They they knew that gosh, I'm gonna make you know $60,000 at a minimum by coming in and working at this place To build and grow my own family and do those types of things that I want to do We expected them to do a hundred dials per day now The great thing about these leads at Russell's driving in is Because the leads are so qualified and following that same system You're probably gonna have to put in that many dials But but dials are critical and calculating what those dials need to be Based upon the leads is something that they need to be that that we're gonna talk about and that they need to be aware of You need to be tracking 500 hours per week that that's really not that difficult for somebody to do Especially when you're down through your leads and gosh, you know 50 70 the people don't even pick up the phone. Okay? It's not talking about a lot four hours of talk time every single day now Yes, what do you do with the other? You know the other four hours at your work? I mean Russell. What kind of difference did we have from the top guys that we had on our floor? What what what made them superstars? Why were they so good? There's a lot I mean, there's a lot of reasons why but you look at like you there was a big a big gap Right the top guys guys like you and Robbie or a randieth by that like we're doing, you know You guys are taking home quarter million dollars or more a year in commission So which means you were putting up a million dollars plus by yourselves in personal volume and down the bottom scale yet guys who can't Pay enough it's selling up to feed their family and there's this huge this huge range I I have my philosophies on what makes a superstar, but I'm excited here yours as well but I think a lot of are just people that are that are hungry that That kind of they have the vision you were talking about like they know like this is what I want to do They're going after and they're and they're trying to hit the numbers but You know you get one or two superstars you're pulling a couple million bucks a year off that versus happen to have 60 60 guys, you know you have the whole the whole wide range So so really what this process is is is instead of us Getting all the dead weight which which we carried a lot of dead weight With 60 sales guys, you know we probably had and and this is you know What's the preto principle 80 80 percent of the money was made by 20 percent of the people Okay, uh that that we had a lot of people there that you know We're just dead weight and the the value with the way Russell's driving in the leads and then the way that we were hiring and bringing in people Is is instead of carrying that dead weight. We're just saying hey, you know what it's not worth the headaches It's not worth the problems with uh, you know that other 80 percent of the people Let's just find the very best people bring them in and and and build that and just find the best leads it's kind of like Yeah, you just we we try to get like the the The cream to rise the top right on the lead side on the sell side because now like you know our goal before is seven thousand dollars per week from a person nowadays We're looking we want twenty thirty thousand dollars per person because best leads best people and just everything if Kimberly and Troy Aren't hitting twenty twenty five thousand dollars a week. You know, but but gosh now I got two people to bring in a fifty seventy a hundred grand a week for me gosh We we take that focus a little bit more energy at this part of our business. Yeah Absolutely, yeah, what one of the things that and this happened to us. This is definitely a learning curve for us for us That's still been says this but he says I'd much rather have an empty seat than an empty suit Okay, don't be tied into gosh. I have to hire. I have to hire. I have to hire and hire the wrong person Take the time to hire the right people. Don't don't hire somebody that's empty You know that's not the right point the right position the right place in their lives to actually just fill up a seat in your office Don't hire somebody that says that they've done sales before don't hire something That's your buddy just because they're your buddy and because you like them Hire the right people to put in there and this this is how we this is how you do it Don't don't keep people on board who aren't reaching their numbers Don't let an empty desk motivate you to hire the wrong person now. I Think everybody out there who's ever been in business has at least seen or heard of the disc profile Yeah, what a lot of people have heard about that and I think the disc profile is awesome It's incredible when I'm looking for a salesperson. Okay. I want somebody who's going to be a high D high I Type person I want somebody who's gonna be influential somebody who's gonna be dominant somebody whose results driven somebody who's that friendly enthusiastic optimistic I want all these types of people now you can do a couple of different things you can go out and you can just run an ad for salespeople and You can say hey, I'm looking for salespeople come in and and and I'll hire you you know and we'll test you We'll see if you're a DI and you can go to the you know Disprofile comm and you can pay 36 bucks every single time that you look at possibly hiring somebody to make sure that they're a high D high I Or or we can actually do do a couple of things to weed them out and find them right from the beginning That the very first thing that you do is you've got to put in the right ad It's critical to make sure that that number one and most importantly I'm looking for the right people the right ad to put into this this particular this particular sales position When we wrote our ads we'd write them that'd be something something along these lines It's gonna say hey We're looking for sales superstars if you're not a top performer Don't even call our average performers make between 50 and a hundred thousand dollars a year our superstars make well over $200,000 a year This position is only for the best salespeople Please call Robbie and let me know why we should hire you and then you know you put your phone number in there now This is great because number one It's gonna potentially it's gonna weed out a lot of your customer service people For this position. I don't want somebody who's customer service Russell you've got customer service people don't you very different mindset When we look at the disc profile, yeah, your customer service people are gonna fall in seat as a CNS people right? Yeah, okay, so so it's a totally completely I don't want CNS people I don't want customer service that I think that's what the CNS almost stands for is His customer service in the disc profile. I might get I never heard that before good But but but it really is that that's that's what happens That's a type of people and there's nothing wrong with those types of people We need you just don't want them for your salespeople So so that that's the first thing is placing that ad I love the attitude because like if you look at like a performer like this is gonna make them be like Like just standing like call it like let us know why we should hire you like they're thinking like I need to I got a cell Sky why should hire me like I'm the best you know like you look at like the superstars like that motivates them and other people that aren't that freaks them out they're like They're avoiding that like the plague where the right person is like challenged by that and they want it They I'm a 200,000 our year person and they call me to explain to why I'm then you know why I'm that person and it definitely It definitely attracts the right the right kind of person it weeds them out doesn't it yeah It's really it's just a matter of finding those right people and again Instead of us carrying a bunch of dead weight instead of us having you know 55 guys that are dead weight and five guys that are making money You know it really just finds the very very best people so so the next thing that happens okay, I so I've got my phone number there and You know there's a lot of different places you can place the ad we had tons and tons of success with placing the ad on craigslist We we did traditional newspaper advertising another another place is that if you have current team members and you know somebody that Gosh, they might be a high D a high. I don't just hire somebody because They you know that they're they're a current team member, but but don't be afraid to bonus your current team members for bringing in the right people We had a lot of a lot of referrals and some of our best people You know Ben came because because he knew Randy because Ben and Randy and Ben was a great Ben was a suit sales superstar for us Derek came because because he knew and so it was just finding don't be afraid to bonus those current team members You know you spend time with people that are similar to people you like and usually super stars hang out with other superstars They always talk about like your incomes the average of your five closest friends And so most sales people know other good sales people they hang out with them They're selling each other on we'll move you go to you know all that kind of stuff and so it's good all the time Look for that The the other thing too when when we look for this and you're not necessarily placing the ad here But but something that's critical always always always be on the lookout for talent I've hired people because I've walked into Best Buy and I get somebody who I know and I understand that But just by talking with them that they're gonna be a high D a high I personality Okay, and and what I will do with those types of people is is I'll give them my business card Okay, and I'll refer them to that ad. Hey, I've got an ad on Craigslist You know, I might even have a link directly to the ad on my business card. Okay, I've got this call this number Okay, look at this. It's something, you know That that possibly you want to take a look at and you want to move forward Always be on the lookout whether it's Best Buy a furniture store Nordstrom's there's so many people out there that are great sales people that Gosh, you take a guy at Best Buy who has that ability to make 200 grand a year and he's making 10 bucks an hour at Best Buy You can change his life and do some incredible things for a young kid who wants to do some of those things The whole way that I got into this industry in the first place What was because somebody found me selling cameras at a camera store? Long long time ago, but that's that's how I found out about this industry What was that somebody somebody gave me a business card and they said call me? Okay? I I think there's some potential here with you I think there's some things that you can do and you know It's it's turned into what I've done is my profession because somebody was out looking as he was going in and buying a camera for somebody So so now that I've placed the ad I've got it there that the next thing I want to do is I want to have my phone interview and I'm gonna I'm gonna for warn you guys that you might have some your HR people that might freak out a little bit about this phone interview Because it's not traditional That the very first thing to do is I get on the phone with them and I just ask him some very very basic Questions interview questions like what's your experience? What's your education? How'd you perform in your last sales job? Just I just want to get him talking and I want to listen to their energy Their stage presence of their voice I want to hear how they sound on the other end of the phone because that's what your prospects are going to be hearing I Want to hear that I want to know and understand what that is Then what I do is is I ask him just a very simple question That it talks about in the interview or in the ad I say Russell tell me why you're a top performer and and all of a sudden, you know It's kind of back to that old, you know You walk into a sales job and they have a pencil and they say hey Sell me on why I should buy this pencil, okay? And they're kind of thinking that in the back of their heads, okay that oh, I've got to sell myself on on why I'm so good Now this is the part where you find your D and your I personalities, okay? it's really the defining moment in the entire interview process and This is where I'm gonna tell you this takes some time to find the right people the right sales superstars But don't hire an empty suit just because you have an empty seat So they take time and and they just build themselves up and they they say how great and how wonderful they've been and how they've Just been top performers and how they just excelled at every aspect in every job and you know I I've never I've never had somebody on a on a resume for a sales job or talking to them Just be like yeah, I don't get along well with people and I don't work with I'm not friendly You know, okay that the people people want to know and understand and they just build themselves up So they've got this great big ego and and then what you do is you just crush their ego You just you just and and this sounds harsh, but but what I do is is I reject them No, what they say, right? I I don't care if I'm like oh my gosh. I got to hire this guy This guy is perfect. This is the most wonderful sales trainer. I mean it doesn't matter what they say, okay? I reject him and say gosh I'm I'm just not hearing superstar here. No matter what no matter what they say, okay? Uh, and and and you get a couple of different a couple of different responses Okay, the the one response and the majority people that respond say oh Okay, well, well, thanks. Thanks for your time. Okay now obviously if I'm doing phone sales and Somebody just simply says hey you just told me how good you are. I don't think you are that good Um Sorry and they say okay and they hang up the phone. What's going to happen the first time they're on the phone with your prospects and the prospect says Ah, gosh, I'm not really sure about you. I'm not really sure about your product The the the prospect is going to say oh well. Okay. Well wish you the best. Do you want my business card? Okay? Uh, that that that's exactly what what's going to happen with that prospect. They're not going to be a sales superstar They're not that d. They're not that I personality that you're looking for Now on on the flip side that the first time that this happened to me Uh, and it's kind of interesting. We're talking with kimberley Uh, uh a little a little bit about this and and kimberley when when we did this she said Are are you not paying attention? Did you not hear what I just said? The the first time that somebody did this to me it uh, we went through this whole process And and he said gosh Robbie, you know, I I'm I'm 21 22 years old. Robbie. I'm just I'm just not hearing, you know superstar I'm not hearing top performer and and I said well apparently you want to make less money and and and the the the uh, the hr person was like, okay Hey, we we want you to we want you to take the next interview. Okay. All of a sudden because If I'm that afraid of rejection I'm not going to be a good sales candidate. I'm not going to work out well And and what that does is immediately within five minutes of a phone interview I found somebody who's my d my i personality Chances are that them falling out of that category are are pretty small now So so we listen to their response and their response needs to be Do you want to make less money? Are you you know, are you stupid? I've had people tell me that before Are are you stupid? You're you're exactly who I want. Okay. I've had other people say are you deaf? Did you not hear what I just said? Okay, I Want some ego here. I want them to tell me that Because when they tell me that I I know that I'm getting somebody that if If if I've got them on the phone and somebody says I don't think you know for whatever reason or another now This isn't for me. No, I want to wait on it. No, I I need to think about it You know, it's too much money The first time they get rejected. Are they just going to hang up the phone? Are they going to say? Oh, okay? Well, I'm gonna I'm going to dial another lead Russell or are they going to say no Apparently you didn't hear what I just said Okay, and and we walk them through that process that gives you the ability to really find the right people that you want and Finding the right people with the right leads all of a sudden two people can do what 60 people were doing before It makes it makes all the difference in the world So so once you find your di personality type now I'm also going to tell you something that that I think is very very valuable once I've got to this point And I found that person that says gosh Robbie are you deaf? Okay, I still want to and at that point I think it's worthwhile to bring them in and to pay the 36 bucks You can get bulk discounts on a disc per personality profile I I think it's worth that in the hiring process to take just a minute Go ahead and do a disc personality profile with them and just make sure that they really fit into that di personality I I haven't ever had somebody that has gone through that process and Hasn't been a di per high di personality But but it's a nice thing for you to have because the better that you can communicate with your salespeople But the better the better everything is going to move. I remember chat homes. I was doing a project with him and I had my initial call with him and he's like, yeah, sounds really good Take this profile and I'll call you back and he forced me before I could do a second call on this project Take the profile because he didn't want to work with me unless I had I was Dean and I as well And so I think it's I think it's key like Sometimes people can fake their way through an interview or whatever It's hard to beat that test because it doesn't make any sense when you're taking it. You know what I mean? And um, It's worth it's worth the the investment after that point It well and and not only that you know now now chat homes He knows how to talk and how to communicate with whether you're d and i and s to see personality Now chat homes knows how he can relate to you and how he can motivate you to to to accomplish whatever it is He wants because the better I can communicate with somebody Uh, the more effective our relationship is going to be okay, and so it allows you to be able to do this I bring the people in we do it. We do a disc task explain the overall business We set clear cut expectations and guidelines They know from day one what's expected of them makes all the difference in the world The last thing in the world you want to do with the sales guy is you want to bring them in You want to get them all set up and all all accustomed to to something And and and then and then change things on them Uh for for whatever reason or another sales guys do not do well with change Make sure that they that you've got the expectations and the guidelines set out from day one and they do wonderful things for you um That the last thing as far as actually hiring and and I do this every time I interview somebody is I want to make sure I understand what makes that individual tick Why they're here what's motivating them because we all have our motivating factors Uh, you know, you guys all saw that house that russell's looking at okay that that that huge beautiful house Okay, that's my motivation I know what russell's motivation is and and and if I come in and I talk to russell I I know enough about russell because we've been friends for long enough that I know that his family is a huge motivation to him And and and if I can understand that these are things that are important to russell I know how to better communicate with russell. I know the things that he's looking for Whether it's a car whether it's a house whether it's spending more time with their family coaching their kids football game and We talk a lot about these types of things that that we do with the prospects throughout the sales process You guys will see that in the set in the closed script with with those videos But but the exact same thing happens with your salespeople if I know when I understand What motivates my sales guys? I can help them to achieve it and it goes back to that old zig-ziggler quote I can have whatever it is. I want my life if I help enough people get exactly what it is that they want And so if I look at my sales guy and I say hey, I I know and understand what motivates you what drives you I know where you want to be I I know that that kimberley wants to to homeschool her her child when when he turns five I know that troi wants an rv and he wants to go to all the national parks I know these things that motivate them now all of a sudden I can do things and I can work within those parameters To help motivate my sales guys at the same time Learn what makes each individual on your team what tick why they tick Why they're there what's going to help them to build and grow their own personal lives and when you take that As as the owner as the entrepreneur and they know and understand that gosh i'm i'm here to help kimberley Uh, you know eventually be able to Homeschool her son or i'm here to help Troi get that rv or i'm here to help randy to to be able to To to golf all over the country or i'm here to help rustle Get that dream whatever it is it doesn't matter if I know what makes them tick and they know that i'm interested in them And that i'm there to help them to reach And exceed and facilitate and help them with their goals. It builds long term Success it builds longevity with your employees It's something that that one of the reasons that rustle and I we're still doing stuff together Is rustle learned a long time ago what types of things make me tick and that that's that's why even even after our call center you know Went away that that we're still doing stuff together is because rustle took the time to no one understand those types of things with me Know what makes your each individual sales guy? On your team no one and help them understand what makes them tick so so that there's there's really uh Um One more key factor that I really want to talk about uh, and that's uh, that's how to deal with Uh Top performer, okay This is important for the entrepreneurs in the room This this is critical because so many entrepreneurs that I've run into Uh are great entrepreneurs. They they they've built great businesses And you know they have no idea they have no clue of how to deal with A top tier salesperson and and what happens a lot of the times they just butt heads and and there there's problems and Russell we ever have budding heads about the sales floor and that never happened, right? So it's amazing like because someone starts making a lot of money they And they are producing a ton of money And they are responsible for a lot of it but in their mind like Like without them you're useless and so it's like this thing where you're like no I'm like this is my business I create and they're like but I'm the one bringing in the money and it causes um definite, uh I don't even know the right word for it, but there's definitely issues that come up Especially when you have two or three or 60 people Um magnifies the problem. So I had an awesome opportunity to learn and grow a lot through that process Um, and I think that it's important though people know because otherwise you can get steamrolled people come in like Hey, I should be getting 50 commission and what do you say like, you know, whatever it is Like you you've got to understand how to how to how to be prepared for that because it will happen It with without especially with hiring Uh, expect it if if it if this doesn't happen you got the wrong guys You haven't hired d and i personalities. So you you've hired the wrong types of people. You want this to happen. Okay? um So there's a couple of things and top producers have a number of attributes that can translate Uh into restlessness. They're original. They're intelligent. They're full of ego And they generally get frustrated in most companies and end up starting their own company that they A lot of the times they have the same ideas the same mentality as the entrepreneur And and there's a way to work with them and to reign that in to pull that back To to make sure that you're working with the right types of people um, so This is going to sound funny. Okay, but the key to working With a top performer is never ever tell them no Now russell, I tell you that and you never ever tell your top performers. No russell's sitting there and like i'm gonna be broke I'm never gonna make any money Okay, uh, obviously Okay, uh, what one of the things to to make sure that you you understand realize is that just because you're not telling them No, it doesn't mean you're telling them. Yes either You've got to redirect their energy or give them some hurdles to jump through i'm gonna go through just a couple of examples Uh that that we can talk about and and how it'll work So let's say I have my top performer come in and he says gosh, you know what russell if we had better communication between all of our departments Uh, I I believe our sales would increase. Okay. Now first of all if if that that kind of sounds like a good thing, right? Gosh, let's all communicate better. We're all gonna make more money, right? Well, if if all of a sudden if I just tell my top performer. Yeah, sure run with it. That's great The top performer He starts taking that and and guess what you give them an inch they take a mile They they keep running with it run with it run with it and and they ask so so can I put that program together for you? So so so all of a sudden we we think as you know the entrepreneur as the business owner Well, of course I want better I I want better communication between all my departments and it just it just obviously it makes sense That If we had better communication, I'd make I'd make more money, right? Okay, so so so we look at that and we think of that and and the entrepreneur they say well sure Yeah, go run with it again if you do that you start you start to build a foundation with that top performer Uh that that they're just gonna keep running and they're gonna keep running running And so so the way to deal with it is gosh that is such a great idea Tell you what you increase sales Three you get three additional sales each month for the next three months and I'll let you run with it Now what I've done is is I've I've taken the control back. Okay. I haven't told my top performer. No, okay In fact, I've told him yes. I've told him yes. I want you to create that program for me But but instead of just saying yes go run with it I've given him something to do something a hurdle to jump over a hoop to go through and and he understands when he realized I'm still the guy that's in control Of the entire process another example is The uh, you know your your top performer comes in says hey these leads they They're terrible. Does that ever happen with the sales guy? I was like the worst conversation. These leads are no good Same leads you called last week. They're no they're no different. Okay What's what's changed your mindset? Okay between your ears the only issue? Absolutely, okay You know or they might say gosh the the promo that that we're running it's it's terrible And you know that the the thing the thing that can happen and it was it's so interesting when you put it back on The the the sales guy, uh, you know if they didn't like say the way that the ad copy was written or whatever it is But the very first thing to do is don't get defensive with a top performer You you start getting defensive and they they can see that I don't know that smoke coming out of your ears, you know, they see that well Because they're a top performer because they're dominant type types of personalities They're gonna fire right back and and nobody ever gets anywhere when you start arguing you start getting defensive and you're both defensive Nobody wins you both lose is is what happens But but something just uh something come back and say I I designed the one that you're criticizing or you know I got those leads that you're criticizing but where would you like to get leads from? Okay, what and so now all of a sudden You know I I take ownership for it that they understand that but at the same token I tell them gosh I I put it back on them and and I let them take some responsibility for it as well And and what happens because the sales guys don't know how to write a better promo piece The sales guys don't know how to drive leads in the sales guys understand and realize that gosh It really is in between my my ears It's it has nothing to do with the leads. It has everything to do with me But but by getting by just saying hey, you know, where where do you think we ought to get it from? And by not getting defensive makes a world of difference with your sales guys with your top performers uh, the the next one just uh, you know a good way to to uh Drive and motivate a top performer comes in and he says gosh, I I could definitely increase sales Uh, if if we do it if we do x y You know what that is awesome. I totally think you can too, but let me tell you what talk is cheap I I want to see it. Let's see those numbers on that board. How about next time I come down tomorrow That that you've got those numbers on the board that you talked about and I'll believe you what will run with it But but talk's cheap. Let's let's let's see it actually happen Let's see it actually get put together and and and that that scoreboard that competition You know Russell talks about I'll I'll bet rust I could ask about guys that you wrestled in high school and maybe even junior high That that maybe beat you a cup you still remember what the score is we we talked about that and talk is cheap You all believe it when I see it and that competitive attitude that they'll say well, okay, I'll show you okay You want them to have that ego you want them to have that competitive attitude That that's what makes the difference. Yeah, you know one or two things will happen one They do and they make more money or two they don't and they shut up You know these looks like you win both ways It's it's a I I want somebody to come in and be a big talker and make me a lot of money all day long You can talk and tell me how great you are and I'll listen to you just just just keep producing Okay, um a couple of things the more you challenge top performer Uh, the more you encourage them to overachieve that the more challenges that they have The the the more obstacles the more hurdles that that they have to jump through that the more they're gonna they're going to achieve for you The last thing and again with a top performer top performers are very very ego driven When they achieve things Don't be in a position Uh that you don't remember to compliment them. They want that they need that they thrive I I thrive on on compliments. I I want them I want you to tell me how it worked out and and especially when something was good Uh, you know, I I I came in and did that training with Russell and gosh, they're I I must text Russell, you know Every week for two weeks. How are they doing because because I want to I I want that I thrive on it. I love it I So do your top performers. They want to hear how good they're doing and and don't don't don't over compliment them But when they reach and exceed their goals make sure that they know that you've noticed make sure that you compliment them you know and and It can be simple things can be an extra bonus. It can be it can just be like, hey great job I saw that you you you blew your numbers away last week. They want that they need it. They thrive on it And in a lot of respects it it makes a bigger difference than that than even a paycheck sometimes that that that yeah It was amazing. Uh, you know, we we would bring in, uh, you know CDs or dvds and you know, we we would we would uh Use those with with our sales guys when somebody exceeded their their sales performance and They didn't care that they just made a thousand dollars But but that I was complimenting him as giving me a new seat You know, you give him an iTunes gift card or something or or or you know, it's amazing ward. Yeah, it's the yeah I Absolutely, I I have a tv that that I I won and and and I tell everybody You know, it's it's a it's a flat screen tv that I won, you know But before I even started working with Russell, uh, and and anybody who yeah I won that that that's still a source of pride for me because it's something that That showed that I accomplished something and I won something They they they want that they they want that recognition They want that compliment and they want it more than just in the score the scoreboard does a great job But when they reach and exceed their goals make make sure that you you go that extra mile to compliment them and let them know that And you know that that's really as far as getting things set up the basic process the mindset the ideology Ideology that you need to have Uh, but behind working with the sales guy working with the top performers hiring and bringing them in and building a call center It's a real simple thing If you if you follow those steps that we talked about that we walked through Russell if we would have if we would have done things that way Six years ago when when I first met you uh We we probably never would have gone through the headaches and the hassles and the and you and I would have would have continued to make a Ton of money together because because the headaches and the hassles aren't there. Are they yeah No, we definitely wanted a lot Hopefully for you guys this will shortcut a lot of pain that we had to go through to kind of figure out The the right path and it's fun now that I haven't had a chance kind of rebuild it after knowing these things and Instructing in the right way how much more simple and smooth and easy things have been um and hopefully this will shortcut that that headache for a lot of you guys because You're right after you have those things in place and you understand that you understand how to work with the people the right way and Um and how to motivate them. Uh, they want to perform for you. They want nothing pleases them more than to to make you happy. It's pretty cool. They love it Alrighty Awesome. Well, that's the first uh, obviously the first thing for you guys entrepreneurs now And there's a lot of other stuff in this package for you guys The biggest one really quickly to talk about the the sex script and the closed scripts They know what to uh to do with that with their with their people Um, if we kind of cover that really quickly it'd be helpful The the next part of the video that that you guys are going to see you're going to see the set script and the closed script and And we've walked through this and I've walked through it also with with your sales people that they're going to see this My recommendation would be number one for you guys to sit down and obviously watch the rest of the videos at least once Uh, I I don't think that it's something you guys need to learn and you guys need to know in in depth and detail Uh, I I always compare it kind of with the entrepreneur that that's kind of like being a general contractor Uh, the general contractor doesn't have to know how to frame and and you know Roof the house and and put the plumbing and the electrical work in into the the house But but but he has to he has to know that gosh that that wiring that that it looks right that Gosh the framing that it looks right and that wall is supposed to be there and that yes that the roofing got done And so so to have a good grasp on how it all works how it's all put together The the next videos are gonna gonna dive into a lot of detail on exactly how the set process works how the closed process works And and so I would recommend watching that at least once as as the business owner The the next thing that I would say too is that for your first couple I'd say probably three to four weeks worth of trainings Um, I I would have your salespeople Obviously number one first day that they're hired that they're ready once you've gone through that process You've got the right people in the room to sit down and to watch the the training videos to watch the set video to watch the closed video And week two to do the exact same thing And and and at the end of that To write down what they learned that was different because they're gonna have a whole Different experience a whole brand new set of results that they're gonna see and they're gonna experience because they've been on the phone for a week So so so Monday Monday week one you do that Monday week two you do that And and then ask them how are they gonna apply the things that they learned week two to what they learned going in do the same thing the third week But by the fourth week they should have a pretty good handle I I would still recommend them going back over that training watching those videos Seeing and understanding those things that we talked about at least over time What once every couple of months the reason behind it? I I've heard and been over and gone through these trainings on the set on the script Literally thousands of times. I've trained on them had other people train me on them And every single time that I've trained on them Uh, maybe I don't learn something new but I remember something that gosh I didn't do that last week and that's such a great thing to say that's such a great thing to Put out there to work with them that I remember that and I bring that back up And and all of a sudden that next week it gets me another sale Uh, and so by going back over that and going back to the basics all the time. Hey guys, this is a football Uh going back to hey, this is this is how we make money. Uh, I remember when I played football and if we'd have a bad game Uh, my my coach wouldn't come in he wouldn't put in a whole bunch of you know fancy trick plays or anything like that He'd go back to guys. This is how we block. This is how we tackle. This is how we run This is how we throw the ball. This is how we catch the ball all the basics And so so one of the things that you've got to look at and you've got to understand is that that by always going back to the basics And remembering what got them to where they're at Uh, it makes all the difference in the world. Kimberly and Troy when I sat down with them yesterday said gosh We've been calling for a couple of months now and now that I've had that experience That this this is great because because we went back over a lot of the same things we did on day one and and Troy walks out And what's the what's the first call you get from Troy? Yeah, $8,000 later $8,000 sale that all of a sudden, you know by by just remembering those things all the time And going back to the basics it makes all the difference in the world You think about any kind of training, you know like you you go you get a new skill Immediately you apply it like we had the next day after you came in 36,000 bucks Just boom and then and then people get cocky or whatever it is And they're like, oh, I know I do this and they started like improvising and start changing it And you watch any kind of skilled jumps like this and it drops and drops and drops and drops And for most people they don't retrain and just kind of it'll keep going till eventually it just kind of dies out Whereas you look at like a good sales a good training system It you have that initial training you get the this the the initial surge of success It starts stabilized starts down falling into the second You know you retrain on it again retrain on and look at it over a year period of time You're going like this But if you just do it once and then it fades and it drops then you're you got this little thing and it dies and it It ends and so, you know when we had 60 salespeople we you did trainings every single week Back to the fundamentals back to the fundamentals and it's going to be annoying for these guys sometimes because like I don't want to go through it again But they need to keep going through and consistently going through it because it brings them back at grounds It re gives them the tools and the things they need and you'll just keep seeing that just jump and jump and jump So I think that's a that's a big key is use the tools we're giving you guys Because that's how you keep people progressing and getting better and better and not just Stabilizing and then dying and we want these things obviously to work for you guys long term So if if Vince on Barty is telling professional football players What a football is this is a football just so you know, okay, this is the goal line You know There's so much that we can learn from that and If we're not going back to those basics, we're we're they're forgetting what the football is They're forgetting what what basic things they need to be doing on a day in and day out basis If you're not continually training on them and be be pigheaded about your training Train, you know, have your training schedule and that's what we're doing is is we're making sure that you've got that training schedule That's put together for your salespeople Be pigheaded about making sure that they're going through it and even after they've gone through all the sales training Guess what go through it again go through it again Go through they will find things that they learn things that they pick up Things that they forgot that they were doing that that are going to help them to to remember and to get more sales and to produce More money for you and for them Awesome. Thanks Robbie. This has been awesome and excited for all you guys to go on to the next step and get your people trained Awesome. Thanks guys
